Added بِ at the start to show this is done 'with peace' (relates to ٱدْخُلُوهَا - 'enter it')
Added two dots under the last letter (tanwin) and changed the long 'aa' shape to show it describes the way something is done
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are close by
The alif is written with a special mark (dagger alif) to show distance

steps.m: that (pointing to something far and specific)
steps.n: Added لِكَ at the end to make it more specific and formal. This word is pointing to يَوْمُ ٱلْخُلُودِ (the Day of Eternity) mentioned right after it.
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukoon) to the root letters to form the common noun meaning 'day'
Added ُ at the end to show this word tells us what 'that' refers to in the sentence 'that is the day of eternity'
The letter ي lost its dots but kept the same meaning and pronunciation. The word still means 'day' and describes 'that'.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding ُ on first and second letters and و between them. This makes it describe the state of lasting forever.
Added ٱلْ to point to this specific eternal state, which describes the day mentioned in the ayah (يَوْمُ)
Added ِ at the end to show this word describes the day (يَوْمُ) mentioned before it, making it mean 'day of eternal state'
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: for them/they have (what يَشَآءُونَ they wish)
steps.n: Added هُم (them) to show who the preposition is referring to. This word connects to يَشَآءُونَ (they wish) in the ayah, showing what they have or what is for them.
Basic form of the word 'ما' which is used to mean 'what' or 'whatever'. In this ayah, it's the object (thing being acted upon) in relation to the verb يَشَآءُونَ (they desire).
Added shadda (doubled م) to emphasize the meaning, making it stronger - like saying 'whatever' instead of just 'what'.
Added fatha vowels and combined letters. The middle root letter ي becomes ا in this pattern.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and changed final vowel to ُ

steps.m: they want/wish (what they want in this life)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action. The long alif (آ) is used for emphasis.
Combines the preposition في (in) with the feminine pronoun ها (it/her)
This is the base word لَدَى which indicates possession or location
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the next part
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'us/our'

steps.m: and with us/and in our possession (connected to مَّا يَشَآءُونَ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about what they desire
Added kasra to show this is about receiving the action
Adding م at start turns 'was increased' into 'something that is increased'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it indefinite - meaning 'a/any increase' rather than 'the increase'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: And how many (connects to أَهْلَكْنَا - We destroyed)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it. This word introduces a question about the many nations that were destroyed, connecting to the verb أَهْلَكْنَا (We destroyed).

Adding أ makes the verb causative - from 'to perish' to 'to cause to perish/destroy'. This is a verb, so it does not take case endings.
نا means 'we' when attached to verbs. This is a complete verb form, not in a case relationship with other words.

steps.m: to be in front of, to precede

steps.n: Changes from root to noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before them (referring to the previous nations mentioned in وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا - 'And how many We destroyed')
steps.n: Added هُم which means 'them' (masculine plural). This refers back to the destroyed nations mentioned earlier in the verse.

Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action. This verb describes what the people mentioned in يَقُولُونَ (they say) are afraid of.

steps.m: to warn or promise (especially about consequences)

steps.n: Root letters joined together to make a noun meaning 'a promise' instead of the action 'to promise'
Added ي between second and third letters to show intensity of the warning
Added kasra at the end to show possession. This is what is being feared in the phrase 'who fears My warning'
